qids.subject: Databases
qids.prose: Which of the following is/are correct? A. An SQL query automatically eliminates duplicates B. An SQL query will not work if there are no indexes on the relations C. SQL permits attribute names to be repeated in the same relation D. None of the above

qids.subject: Databases
qids.prose: Consider the set of relations EMP (Employee-no. Dept-no, Employee-name, Salary) DEPT (Dept-no. Dept-name, Location) Write an SQL query to: a. Find all employees names who work in departments located at `Calcutta' and whose salary is greater than Rs.50,000. b. Calculate, for each department number, t

qids.subject: Databases
qids.prose: Consider the set of relations EMP (Employee-no. Dept-no, Employee-name, Salary) DEPT (Dept-no. Dept-name, Location) Write an SQL query to: Calculate, for each department number, the number of employees with a salary greater than Rs. 1,00,000

qids.subject: Databases
qids.prose: Given relations r(w, x) and s(y, z) the result of select distinct w, x from r, s is guaranteed to be same as r, provided. A. r has no duplicates and s is non- empty C. s has no duplicates and r is non- empty
